source.notes: Imported from Azure DevOps work item 6445

observedSteps: User Story
As a guest user, I want to access the Home page and use the available navigation actions, so that I can navigate to login, customer signup, clean survey, and site survey workflows.
Application Context
The workflow starts by directly navigating to the application Home page at:
http://localhost/washtabui/home

The Home page is expected to load in guest state without authentication. The workflow validates guest-state navigation options and transitions from the Home page to downstream pages through UI controls.
Entry Conditions
Application is launched and accessible.
User is not authenticated and is operating in guest state.
User navigates directly to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
Home page load completes successfully before interaction begins.
Workflow Scope
This workflow covers:
Guest-state Home page accessibility
Visibility and enabled-state validation for primary Home page controls
Navigation behavior from the Home page to:
Login page
Customer signup/customer-search page
Vehicle survey page
Site survey page
URL transition validation for each supported navigation path
Primary Navigation Journey
Launch the application.
Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
Verify the Home page loads in guest state.
Verify the following controls are visible and enabled:
Person/Profile button
Back button
Notification button
Home button
Customer signup button
Clean survey button
Site survey button
Select the Person/Profile button and verify navigation to:
/washtabui/login?data=undefined
Return to the Home page in guest state.
Select the Customer signup button and verify navigation to a URL containing:
customer-search
Select the Clean survey button and verify navigation to a URL containing:
vehicle-survey
Select the Site survey button and verify navigation to a URL containing:
survey?data
Approved Test Data
Entry URL:
Expected login URL:
Expected customer search URL fragment:
Expected vehicle survey URL fragment:
Expected survey URL fragment:
Page / Component Elements
Home Page:
Target Pages:
Customer-search page
Vehicle-survey page
Survey page
Navigation controls should reuse existing shared application navigation behavior where applicable rather than duplicating ownership logic within downstream workflows.
Business Rules
The Home page must be accessible in guest state.
Home page navigation controls must be visible and enabled before interaction.
Selecting the Person/Profile button must navigate to the login page URL:
Selecting the Customer signup button must navigate to a URL containing:
Selecting the Clean survey button must navigate to a URL containing:
Selecting the Site survey button must navigate to a URL containing:
Validation Expectations
Home page loads successfully at the specified entry URL.
Guest-state Home page renders without requiring authentication.
All listed Home page buttons are visible.
All listed Home page buttons are enabled and interactive.
Clicking each navigation control results in the expected page transition.
Resulting URLs contain the expected values or fragments.
Transition Expectations
Successful application launch transitions the user to the Home page in guest state.
Clicking the Person/Profile button transitions the user from Home to the Login page.
Clicking the Customer signup button transitions the user from Home to the customer-search page.
Clicking the Clean survey button transitions the user from Home to the vehicle-survey page.
Clicking the Site survey button transitions the user from Home to the survey page.
If navigation fails, the user remains on the Home page or does not reach the expected destination URL.
Resource Reuse Guidance
Reuse the Home page resource and shared navigation components across manual and automation assets.
Reuse common button validation logic for visibility and enabled-state checks.
Reuse navigation verification resources for URL transition validation.
Downstream workflows for login, customer-search, vehicle-survey, and survey pages should own their internal page validations; this workflow should validate only successful navigation entry.
Downstream Automation Guidance
Use the provided Home page URL as the standardized workflow entry point.
Execute all validations in guest state.
Validate both UI state and resulting URL transitions.
Reuse approved URL fragments consistently across manual and automation assets.
Keep navigation verification isolated from downstream page business validations.
Treat each navigation path as an independent reusable workflow branch.
Acceptance Criteria
Given the user launches the application and navigates to http://localhost/washtabui/home, when the page loads, then the Home page is displayed successfully in guest state.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Person/Profile button is visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Back button is visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Notification button is visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Home button is visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Customer signup button is visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Clean survey button is visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Site survey button is visible and enabled.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Person/Profile button, then the application navigates to a URL equal to /washtabui/login?data=undefined.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Customer signup button, then the application navigates to a URL containing customer-search.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Clean survey button, then the application navigates to a URL containing vehicle-survey.
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Site survey button, then the application navigates to a URL containing survey?data.

externalContext.title: Home Feature - Guest State
externalContext.description:
1. User Story
 
 As a guest user, I want to access the Home page and use the available navigation actions, so that I can navigate to login, customer signup, clean survey, and site survey workflows.
 
 2. Application Context
 
 The workflow starts by directly navigating to the application Home page at:
 http://localhost/washtabui/home
 
 The Home page is expected to load in guest state without authentication. The workflow validates guest-state navigation options and transitions from the Home page to downstream pages through UI controls.
 
 3. Entry Conditions
 
 - Application is launched and accessible.
 - User is not authenticated and is operating in guest state.
 - User navigates directly to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
 - Home page load completes successfully before interaction begins.
 
 4. Workflow Scope
 
 This workflow covers:
 - Guest-state Home page accessibility
 - Visibility and enabled-state validation for primary Home page controls
 - Navigation behavior from the Home page to:
 - Login page
 - Customer signup/customer-search page
 - Vehicle survey page
 - Site survey page
 - URL transition validation for each supported navigation path
 
 5. Primary Navigation Journey
 
 1. Launch the application.
 2. Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home.
 3. Verify the Home page loads in guest state.
 4. Verify the following controls are visible and enabled:
 - Person/Profile button
 - Back button
 - Notification button
 - Home button
 - Customer signup button
 - Clean survey button
 - Site survey button
 5. Select the Person/Profile button and verify navigation to:
 - /washtabui/login?data=undefined
 6. Return to the Home page in guest state.
 7. Select the Customer signup button and verify navigation to a URL containing:
 - customer-search
 8. Return to the Home page in guest state.
 9. Select the Clean survey button and verify navigation to a URL containing:
 - vehicle-survey
 10. Return to the Home page in guest state.
 11. Select the Site survey button and verify navigation to a URL containing:
 - survey?data
 
 6. Approved Test Data
 
 - Entry URL:
 - http://localhost/washtabui/home
 - Expected login URL:
 - /washtabui/login?data=undefined
 - Expected customer search URL fragment:
 - customer-search
 - Expected vehicle survey URL fragment:
 - vehicle-survey
 - Expected survey URL fragment:
 - survey?data
 
 7. Page / Component Elements
 
 Home Page:
 - Person/Profile button
 - Back button
 - Notification button
 - Home button
 - Customer signup button
 - Clean survey button
 - Site survey button
 
 Target Pages:
 - Login page
 - Customer-search page
 - Vehicle-survey page
 - Survey page
 
 Navigation controls should reuse existing shared application navigation behavior where applicable rather than duplicating ownership logic within downstream workflows.
 
 8. Business Rules
 
 - The Home page must be accessible in guest state.
 - Home page navigation controls must be visible and enabled before interaction.
 - Selecting the Person/Profile button must navigate to the login page URL:
 - /washtabui/login?data=undefined
 - Selecting the Customer signup button must navigate to a URL containing:
 - customer-search
 - Selecting the Clean survey button must navigate to a URL containing:
 - vehicle-survey
 - Selecting the Site survey button must navigate to a URL containing:
 - survey?data
 
 9. Validation Expectations
 
 - Home page loads successfully at the specified entry URL.
 - Guest-state Home page renders without requiring authentication.
 - All listed Home page buttons are visible.
 - All listed Home page buttons are enabled and interactive.
 - Clicking each navigation control results in the expected page transition.
 - Resulting URLs contain the expected values or fragments.
 
 10. Transition Expectations
 
 - Successful application launch transitions the user to the Home page in guest state.
 - Clicking the Person/Profile button transitions the user from Home to the Login page.
 - Clicking the Customer signup button transitions the user from Home to the customer-search page.
 - Clicking the Clean survey button transitions the user from Home to the vehicle-survey page.
 - Clicking the Site survey button transitions the user from Home to the survey page.
 - If navigation fails, the user remains on the Home page or does not reach the expected destination URL.
 
 11. Resource Reuse Guidance
 
 - Reuse the Home page resource and shared navigation components across manual and automation assets.
 - Reuse common button validation logic for visibility and enabled-state checks.
 - Reuse navigation verification resources for URL transition validation.
 - Downstream workflows for login, customer-search, vehicle-survey, and survey pages should own their internal page validations; this workflow should validate only successful navigation entry.
 
 12. Downstream Automation Guidance
 
 - Use the provided Home page URL as the standardized workflow entry point.
 - Execute all validations in guest state.
 - Validate both UI state and resulting URL transitions.
 - Reuse approved URL fragments consistently across manual and automation assets.
 - Keep navigation verification isolated from downstream page business validations.
 - Treat each navigation path as an independent reusable workflow branch.
 
 13. Acceptance Criteria
 
 - Given the user launches the application and navigates to http://localhost/washtabui/home, when the page loads, then the Home page is displayed successfully in guest state.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Person/Profile button is visible and enabled.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Back button is visible and enabled.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Notification button is visible and enabled.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Home button is visible and enabled.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Customer signup button is visible and enabled.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Clean survey button is visible and enabled.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Site survey button is visible and enabled.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Person/Profile button, then the application navigates to a URL equal to /washtabui/login?data=undefined.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Customer signup button, then the application navigates to a URL containing customer-search.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Clean survey button, then the application navigates to a URL containing vehicle-survey.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user clicks the Site survey button, then the application navigates to a URL containing survey?data.

Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Back button is visible and enabled.

Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the page is displayed, then the Customer signup button is visible and enabled.
